in truth: indeed; truly; in reality; verily (adverbial phrase used to emphasize the truth of a statement or to assert sincerity
very well: all right; okay; certainly; excellently (/formal expression of agreement:  consent:  or acknowledgment
amidst: in the middle of; among; surrounded by (preposition variant of 'amid'; in the midst of)
aught: anything at all; anything whatever; any part:  amount:  or degree (pronoun/noun:  often in negative constructions meaning 'nothing'; also variant of 'ought' as zero from misdivision of 'a naught')
sunder: to separate; to divide; to split apart (verb); 'in sunder' or 'asunder' meaning apart or into pieces (: to put asunder:  rend; common in )
art: (verb) second person singular present indicative of 'be' (thou art = you are;  conjugation); (noun) skill or craft (esp. opposed to nature); learning:  science:  or the liberal arts; practical application of knowledge or experience; magic:  sorcery:  or occult power; artifice:  cunning:  or trickery (all senses :  Merriam-Webster :  and Onions/ Lexicon)
are not: are not (negative form of 'be' in Early Modern English:  often used in questions or emphatic statements without modern contraction
are they not: are they not (rhetorical or tag question form in  English:  equivalent to modern 'aren't they?')
are you not: are you not (/tag question form equivalent to 'aren't you?')
magnificent: grand; splendid; noble; excellent (often with connotations of pomp or show; imposing in appearance)
scarce: scarcely; barely; hardly; with difficulty (adverbial use; also adjective meaning rare or insufficient)
belike: perhaps; probably; it may be; likely (adverb meaning 'it is likely')
ere: before; sooner than; prior to ; earlier than)
ifsoe'er: if ever (contraction/variant of 'if so e'er' or 'ifsoever'; poetic for 'if ever at all')
betwixt: between (preposition; also 'betwixt and between' meaning in an intermediate position)
haply: perhaps; by chance; possibly; luckily (adverb; : by hap or chance)
fare well: farewell; goodbye; may you fare well (parting expression; variant of 'farewell'; from Early Modern English)
behest: command; order; injunction; request (noun; : a command or authoritative bidding)
could not: could not (negative of 'could'; used in Early Modern English without modern contraction)
wight: person; human being; creature (noun:  often with 'poor wight' meaning unfortunate person)
bane: cause of death:  ruin:  or destruction; poison; murder or woe (noun/verb)
dare not: dare not (negative of 'dare'; used formally in Early Modern English)
tarry: to wait; to delay; to linger; to stay (verb; : to remain or wait)
dire: dreadful; terrible; ominous; causing horror (adjective; horrible)
I know not: I know not (for 'I do not know'; common  inversion)
betimes: early; soon; in good time; occasionally (adverb)
lest: for fear that; in order to prevent; that...not (conjunction)
forsooth: indeed; truly; in truth (adverb used often ironically or for emphasis)
hence: from here; from this place/time; therefore; away (adverb)
henceforth: from this time forward; from now on (adverb)
whence: from where; from what place/source; how (adverb/conjunction)
fain: gladly; willingly; with pleasure; compelled or obliged (adverb/adjective; : gladly:  with pleasure)
goodly: handsome; fine; pleasant; considerable in size (adjective/adverb)
farewell: goodbye; a parting wish of well-being (interjection/noun)
must: must (modal verb with same senses as modern: but often in formal or emphatic Early Modern usage)
hail: to greet; to call to; hail (verb/interjection of greeting)
smite: to strike; to hit forcefully; to afflict or punish (verb; to strike with a weapon)
methinks: it seems to me; I think (impersonal verb)
jest: joke; to mock or jeer; a witty remark (noun/verb; also 'in jest' meaning not seriously)
lo: behold; look; see (interjection for drawing attention)
most wise: very wise; wisest (adjective with superlative meaning)
nay: no; not; indeed (adverb for negation or emphasis)
aye: yes; ever; always (adverb for affirmation or eternity)
prithee: I pray thee; please (contraction of 'I pray thee')
fie: shame; for shame (interjection of disgust or reproach)
queer: strange; odd; peculiar (also 'to queer' meaning to spoil)
whither: to where; to what place; whereto (adverb/conjunction)
wend: to go; to proceed; to turn (verb)
most: very; almost; greatest (adverbial use for emphasis)
wish to: desire to; want to (periphrastic for future/intention in Early Modern English)
wilt: (verb) second person singular of 'will' (thou wilt = you will;)
yea: yes; indeed; truly (adverb for affirmation; stronger than 'yes')
assay: to try; to test; to attempt (verb/noun)
bethink: to think; to consider; to devise; to remember (verb; to think of:  consider:  devise)
commend: to entrust; to praise; to recommend (verb)
coz: cousin (abbreviation; used for any relative or intimate)
forfend: to forbid; to avert; to protect from (verb)
thence: from there; from that place/time; therefore (adverb)
recreant: coward; traitor; one who yields; false (noun/adjective)
vanquish: to conquer; to defeat; to overcome (verb)
troth: truth; fidelity; pledge; betrothal (noun; 'by my troth' = truly)
yonder: over there; that place in the distance (adverb/adjective)
yon: over there; that place in the distance (adjective/adverb; variant of 'yonder')
with all speed: quickly; as fast as possible (adverbial phrase)
wit: know; knowledge; understanding; intelligence (verb/noun; 'I wit not' = I do not know)
wit not: have no knowledge (phrase; 'wit not' = I do not know)
beseech: to beg; to implore; to request earnestly (verb)
bespeak: to speak to; to request; to indicate (verb)
sup: to eat or drink (verb); a meal (noun)
cease: to stop; to end; to discontinue (verb); end or stopping point (noun)
quell: to suppress; to put an end to; to subdue (verb)
apart: (Used after a noun or in the predicate) Exceptional, distinct.
awesome: Inspiring awe; especially:  extremely impressive or daunting;
awful: Inspiring awe; especially:  extremely impressive or daunting;.
